ccel.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second
ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.text: For deserters are not called so as all of one kind.
One is wicked, another partially withdraws; but yet true judgments are decreed for both.
So Christ is fought against, even as Cæsar is obeyed.
Seek the refuge of the king, if thou hast been a delinquent.
Do thou implore of Him; do thou prostrate confess to Him: He will grant all things whose also are all our things.
The camp being replaced, beware of sinning further; do not wander long as a soldier through caves of the wild beasts.
Let it be sin to thee to cease from unmeasured doing.
